{"key_event":"Reform-era opening and Tiananmen crackdown","reasoning":"Market reforms, decollectivization, universities, media debate, and legal rebuilding gave households, entrepreneurs, students, and intellectuals more autonomy and mobilizational capacity, but the June 1989 crackdown kept independent society weak overall. The party-state lost some direct command-economy and central fiscal control, yet rebuilt bureaucratic/legal capacity, imposed intrusive population control, and demonstrated overwhelming coercive capacity at Tiananmen.","society_change":1.2,"society_power":3.2,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":8.2}
